summary: Matched a small but structurally useful slice of `melee/ft/ft_081B.c`: the report credits `ft_80082708`, `ft_80085030`, and `ft_80085134` as 100% and improves `ft_80081B38` to roughly 48%. The PR also renamed several `CollData` fields to semantic names (`cur_topn`, `cur_topn_correct`, `prev_topn`, `ecb_lock`) across fighter, item, and map-collision code, added an `ftData_x44_t` layout used by `ft_80081B38`, corrected `mpColl_8004B108` to return `bool`, and converted many still-unmatched `ft_081B` stubs from `NOT_IMPLEMENTED` bodies to decomp placeholder comments.
what_changed: `ft_80081B38` was implemented in C enough to score 48.18%, initializing fighter collision data from `cur_pos`, copying current/previous top-N positions, calling `mpColl_80041EE4`, building ECB joints via `mpColl_80042078`, applying scale to `ft_data->x44` offsets, calling `mpColl_80042374`, setting collision weight, and setting `x128/x12C` when `x104 == 1`.

`ft_80082708` was matched as `EnvironmentCollision_AllowGroundToAir(SpecialAttackGround)`: it moves `prev_topn` to `cur_topn`, copies fighter `cur_pos` into collision data, calls `mpColl_8004B108`, writes back `fp->cur_pos = cd->cur_topn`, and returns `GA_Air` or `GA_Ground` based on the boolean result.
`ft_80085134` was matched by using `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` and setting `self_vel.x` from `x6A4_transNOffset.z * facing_dir` and `self_vel.y` from `x6A4_transNOffset.y`.
`ft_80085030` was already present in C and remained part of the 100% report; surrounding unmatched `NOT_IMPLEMENTED` function bodies were replaced with `/// #symbol` placeholders.
`CollData` fields in `src/melee/lb/types.h` were renamed from positional placeholders: `x4_vec` to `cur_topn`, `x10_vec` to `cur_topn_correct`, `x1C_vec` to `prev_topn`, and `x19C` to `ecb_lock`.

The new `CollData` names were propagated through `mpcoll.c`, fighter-common transitions, fighter ticking, item collision ownership, Link air-catch, Ness yo-yo collision helper comments, and item position code.
`src/melee/ft/types.h` gained `ftData_x44_t`, a 12-field `s16` table layout, and `struct ftData` changed the old `filler_x40[2]` into `_40` plus `ftData_x44_t* x44`.
`mpColl_8004B108` in `mpcoll.h` was corrected from returning `void` to returning `bool`, matching how `ft_80082708` consumes it.
`config/GALE01/scratches.txt` updated scratch statuses for `func_80081B38`, `done__func_80082708`, and `func_80085134` from percentages/100% labels to `OK`.
`asm/melee/ft/ft_081B.s` was updated heavily, including labels and comments changing from scratch-local offsets to real DOL addresses such as `0x80081B38 - 0x8008521C` and `.L_80081C6C`.
smart_moves: Renaming `CollData` top-N position fields made the matched functions much more readable and exposed the current/previous position push pattern used throughout collision code.

The `ftData_x44_t` struct captured a repeated data-table layout of `s16` joint indices and ECB scale parameters, allowing `ft_80081B38` to express indexed `FighterBone` joint loads instead of raw offsets.
Changing `mpColl_8004B108` to `bool` removed a prototype lie and made `ft_80082708`'s ground-to-air return logic natural.
The matched `ft_80082708` uses a scoped temporary `bool fall_off_ledge`; this likely helps produce the desired code shape while documenting the semantic meaning of the return value.
Replacing many `NOT_IMPLEMENTED` stub bodies with `/// #function` placeholders reduced C noise and kept the file ready for incremental decomp without pretending those functions are implemented.
Propagating field renames across item, fighter, and map-collision users avoided local readability gains that would leave inconsistent aliases elsewhere.
decomp_lessons: When a struct field is used pervasively, semantic renames can unlock cleaner matching in multiple files at once; here `cur_topn`, `cur_topn_correct`, and `prev_topn` clarified collision-state movement.
Do not trust old placeholder prototypes: a single return-type correction (`mpColl_8004B108` as `bool`) can be necessary before a small function decompiles cleanly.
Small data-layout discoveries in fighter data (`ftData_x44_t`) are worth upstreaming early because they remove raw-index arithmetic from ECB setup functions.
A low overall file percentage can still produce high-value infrastructure: this PR only reports 3.09% of `ft_081B`, but its struct and prototype cleanup benefits later collision and fighter decomp.

For functions that copy current position into collision data, preserve the original order: previous top-N is saved before current top-N is overwritten, then fighter position is restored from collision after collision processing.
Use typed aliases such as `Fighter_GObj*`, `Fighter*`, `CollData*`, and `GroundOrAir` when they mirror existing project conventions; they make decompiled control flow easier to audit.
naming_conventions: The fighter data table at offset `+0x44` was named `ftData_x44_t` and stored in `struct ftData` as `x44`.
The PR retained address-based function names such as `ft_80081B38`, `ft_80082708`, `ft_80085030`, and `ft_80085134` where no higher-level name was established.
An inline comment gives `ft_80082708` the semantic label `EnvironmentCollision_AllowGroundToAir(SpecialAttackGround)` without renaming the symbol.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Matched functions were tracked through the PR report: `ft_80082708`, `ft_80085030`, and `ft_80085134` at 100%, and `ft_80081B38` at 48.18%.

The asm file changed from scratch-relative offsets like `00000000` and labels like `.L_00000134` to real addresses like `80081B38` and labels like `.L_80081C6C`, indicating the assembly context was regenerated or readdressed alongside source progress.
`ft_80081B38` expresses the ECB setup call with indexed `temp_r11[temp_r29->unkN].joint` arguments, matching the source order implied by the assembly `lha`, `slwi`, and `lwzx` sequence.
`ft_80082708` keeps the collision-result boolean in a local before returning a ternary `fall_off_ledge ? GA_Air : GA_Ground`, a compact way to match the branch-result pattern.
`ft_80085134` matches a short velocity-transfer function by mapping translation offset fields directly into `self_vel` with facing-direction multiplication.
The PR used scratch metadata updates to mark completed functions as `OK`, keeping decomp.me tracking synchronized with repository status.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice, so there is no explicit reviewer feedback to extract.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that further decompile `ft_081B.c` after the `CollData` field renames.
Search for all remaining uses of `cur_topn_correct` to refine its exact semantic meaning.
Audit `ftData_x44_t` field names once more fighter data users are matched.
Look for later renames of `EnvironmentCollision_AllowGroundToAir(SpecialAttackGround)` or `ft_80082708`.
Check whether `ecb_lock` was later renamed or documented with lifecycle semantics.
